title: Trusted-proxy browser WebSocket handshakes could bypass origin validation
description: The resolveHandshakeBrowserSecurityContext function sets enforceOriginCheckForAnyClient to true only when a browser Origin header is present AND no proxy headers are detected (hasBrowserOriginHeader && !hasProxyHeaders). In trusted-proxy mode, legitimate proxy headers (X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP) are always present, causing hasProxyHeaders to be true and enforceOriginCheckForAnyClient to be false. This means browser WebSocket connections arriving through a trusted proxy never have their Origin header validated, allowing cross-site WebSocket hijacking from any origin to gain operator.admin access.
